page: King of the Andals, the Rhoynar, and the First Men
html:
King of the Andals, the Rhoynar, and the First Men
The Iron Throne,
by Marc Simonetti © Penguin Random House Grupo Editorial
Office Ruler of the Seven Kingdoms
Region Seven Kingdoms
Current Holder Tommen I Baratheon
(disputed by Stannis Baratheon, Daenerys Targaryen, and Aegon Targaryen)
Heir Myrcella Baratheon
(disputed by Shireen Baratheon)
First Holder Aegon I Targaryen
Creator High Septon
Created 1 AC

King of the Andals, the Rhoynar, and the First Men is the first title born by the monarch of the Seven Kingdoms of Westeros, who rules from the Iron Throne in the Red Keep of King's Landing.[1][2][3] George R. R. Martin has also used King of the Andals and the Rhoynar and the First Men.[4][5] The monarch's chief advisor is the Hand of the King.[6]

In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, the title is simply King of the Andals and the First Men.

History[ ]

Aegon I Targaryen and Balerion, by Josu Hernaiz ©

Originally crowned as King of All Westeros and Shield of His People by his sister-wife Rhaenys, Aegon I Targaryen abandoned these titles when he was crowned by the High Septon at the Starry Sept of Oldtown as "King of the Andals, the Rhoynar, and the First Men, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms, and Protector of the Realm". This title indicated that Aegon claimed authority over even the Dornish, despite their successful resistance to House Targaryen in Aegon's Conquest[7] and the First Dornish War.[8]

At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Prince Daemon Targaryen crowned his wife, Rhaenyra Targaryen, as Queen of the Andals, the Rhoynar, and the First Men, in opposition to Rhaenyra's half-brother, Aegon II Targaryen.[9] The civil war ended with Rhaenyra and Aegon II both dead and Rhaenyra's son, Aegon III, sitting the Iron Throne.[10]

Recent Events[ ]

A Game of Thrones[ ]

King Robert I Baratheon dies and is succeeded by his son Joffrey I Baratheon,[11] whose biological father is actually Ser Jaime Lannister.

A Clash of Kings[ ]

The Iron Throne is disputed by Joffrey and his uncles, Stannis and Renly Baratheon, during the War of the Five Kings.[12]

A Storm of Swords[ ]

Joffrey is murdered at his royal wedding[13] and is succeeded by his younger brother, Tommen I Baratheon.[14]

A Feast for Crows[ ]

Tommen I Baratheon and his three kittens in front of the Iron Throne, by Magali Villeneuve © Fantasy Flight Games

Arianne Martell plots to crown Tommen's elder sister, Princess Myrcella Baratheon, as Queen of the Andals, the Rhoynar, and the First Men,[15] but her plot is foiled by her father Doran, Prince of Dorne.[16]

A Dance with Dragons[ ]

The Queen of Meereen, Daenerys Targaryen, claims to be Queen of the Andals and the Rhoynar and the First Men.[17]

In Volon Therys, Jon Connington introduces Aegon Targaryen as Aegon, the Sixth of His Name, King of the Andals, the Rhoynar, and the First Men.[18]

Quotes[ ]

In the name of Robert of the House Baratheon, the First of his Name, King of the Andals and the Rhoynar and the First Men, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm, by the word of Eddard of the House Stark, Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North, I do sentence you to die.[4]

—Eddard Stark to Gared

This is the will and word of Robert of House Baratheon, the First of his Name, King of the Andals and all the rest—put in the damn titles, you know how it goes.[19]

—Robert I Baratheon to Eddard Stark

Behind the scenes[ ]

George R. R. Martin has acknowledged that the Westerosi king has similarities with the Holy Roman Emperor.[20]
